title: Environment, Shell State, Startup Files, and Sessions · Incident Repair Bay
summary: Repair a command that works interactively but fails in automation by tracing environment and startup differences instead of adding global hacks. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an environment-diff and startup-resolution notebook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Distinguish shell variables, exported environment entries, positional parameters, and child-process inheritance.
Trace aliases, functions, builtins, hashed commands, PATH search, and explicit paths with type and command evidence.
Identify login, interactive, non-interactive, and remote shell startup paths before editing configuration.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — check-report works in an interactive shell but fails from a service with PATH=/usr/bin:/bin, LANG=C.UTF-8, and HOME=/var/lib/lab. Trace command resolution and startup files without exporting a secret token. Competency probe: Distinguish shell variables, exported environment entries, positional parameters, and child-process inheritance.

Inspect the Shell Variables Environment trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish shell variables, exported environment entries, positional parameters, and child-process inheritance.

content.text: For Shell Variables Environment, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Shell Variables Environment precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace command resolution and startup files without exporting a secret token. Competency probe: Distinguish shell variables, exported environment entries, positional parameters, and child-process inheritance.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Assigning a shell variable automatically exports it to children.
